skill.name: issue-review
skill.description: Guides structured issue review by coordinating problem analysis, codebase investigation, optional git and log analysis, and root cause validation.
skill.summary: Run a structured workflow for bug triage and root cause analysis.
skill.author: DennisLiuCk

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command patterns, one weak cryptography pattern, and a high entropy heuristic. Manual review found these are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code examples, the YAML description key, and multilingual diagram content. No malicious behavior, network exfiltration, executable script, prompt injection attempt, or credential access was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and inline code formatting that describe an issue review workflow. They do not execute shell commands or invoke Ruby backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown fences, report templates, or command-name documentation. I did not find executable Ruby, shell, or script code in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is A YAML Field Name
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at line 3 matches the YAML key named description, not a cryptographic API or algorithm choice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata front matter and contains no cryptographic function, cipher, hash, key, or protocol usage. The finding is a lexical false positive.

low_findings.title: Entropy Heuristic Explained By Multilingual Formatting
low_findings.description: The high entropy heuristic at line 1 appears to be caused by Chinese text, emoji, box drawing characters, and Markdown formatting. No binary blob, encoded payload, or encrypted content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows readable Markdown throughout the file. The unusual byte distribution is explained by Unicode content rather than obfuscation.

low_findings.title: Broad Repository And Log Review Requires User Awareness
low_findings.description: The workflow asks agents to inspect code, git history, and logs during troubleshooting. This is legitimate for issue review but may expose sensitive project data to the active AI tool context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly directs analysis of git history, codebase paths, logs, and screenshots. This is expected behavior, but users should avoid supplying secrets or regulated data.

content.user_title: Review Issues With Root Cause Analysis
content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to investigate bugs, alerts, and user reports. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured triage, code investigation, and root cause reporting.
actual_capabilities: Collects issue symptoms, environment details, reproduction steps, and error messages.
Coordinates problem analysis, codebase investigation, and root cause validation stages.
Conditionally includes git history review when an issue appears after recent changes.
Conditionally includes log and stack trace analysis when error details are provided.
Produces a structured final report with root cause, evidence, priority, and validation steps.
Defines fallback behavior when an analysis stage fails or evidence is incomplete.
limitations: It provides investigation guidance and does not automatically fix code.
It depends on the active AI tool having access to relevant repository files and logs.
It may need additional information when the issue report is incomplete.
It references agent names that may require matching tool support in the host environment.

use_cases.title: Triage production incidents
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to classify symptoms, review logs, inspect relevant code paths, and produce a root cause report.

use_cases.title: Investigate regression reports
use_cases.description: Review recent git changes and affected modules when a bug appears after a release or deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare bug handoffs
use_cases.description: Turn user reports, screenshots, and stack traces into structured findings for the team that owns the fix.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic issue review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a clear bug report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use issue-review to analyze this bug report: [describe the symptom, environment, expected behavior, actual behavior, and reproduction steps].

prompt_templates.title: Analyze an error with logs
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt when stack traces or logs are available
prompt_templates.prompt: Use issue-review to investigate this error. Include the stack trace, relevant logs, timestamp, environment, and what changed before it started.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a suspected regression
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt when a recent change may be involved
prompt_templates.prompt: Use issue-review to find whether a recent change caused this issue. Focus on the affected module, recent commits, release timing, and likely code paths.

prompt_templates.title: Produce an incident-ready report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for team handoff or post-incident review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use issue-review to create a root cause report with evidence, confidence, impacted files, mitigation options, validation steps, and open questions.

output_examples.input: A user reports that checkout fails only after the latest deployment and provides a payment error message.
output_examples.output: The skill separates symptoms from assumptions, checks recent change history, investigates checkout code paths, and reports the most likely root cause with validation steps.

output_examples.input: A service alert includes repeated timeout logs and an affected endpoint name.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes the error pattern, traces the endpoint flow, compares possible causes, and recommends monitoring checks to confirm recovery.

output_examples.input: A QA tester provides reproduction steps but no logs.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies missing evidence, starts codebase investigation from the reproduced path, and lists targeted questions for faster confirmation.

best_practices: Provide symptoms, environment, timestamps, reproduction steps, and recent change context at the start.
Share only logs and screenshots that are needed for investigation, and remove secrets first.
Ask for evidence, confidence, and validation steps before acting on a proposed root cause.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a substitute for tests, monitoring, or human code review.
Do not provide raw secrets, private customer data, or regulated logs without sanitization.
Do not stop at the first hypothesis when the evidence is weak or contradictory.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant through structured issue triage, code investigation, optional log review, and root cause reporting.

faq.question: Does it make code changes?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on analysis, evidence, and repair recommendations. You can ask your coding tool to implement fixes separately.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it for user-reported bugs, production alerts, failed tests, regressions, and unclear technical issues.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the symptom, environment, reproduction steps, error messages, logs, affected files, and recent changes when available.

faq.question: Is it safe to share logs?
faq.answer: Share only relevant logs and remove secrets, tokens, personal data, and regulated information before analysis.

faq.question: Does it require special agent support?
faq.answer: The workflow references named agents. It works best in environments that support task delegation or equivalent manual stages.
